title: conospermum caeruleum subsp. contortum
intro: Conospermum caeruleum subsp. contortum is a subspecies of the Conospermum caeruleum plant, commonly known as the 'Blue Spinecone' or 'Banksia', which is a type of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with this plant include relief from fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as a treatment for skin conditions. The herbal preparations for Conospermum caeruleum subsp. contortum typically involve making a tea from the leaves and branches, or using it as a compress to apply directly to the affected area.
